ci_sentinels.pnpm_test_db_via_t1_proof_gate_ci: PASS -- CI's "T1 Proof Gate" job (proof-gate.yml, job t1-proof, step "C2 — live-DB proof execution") runs `pnpm ci:db-smoke` (pnpm test:db equivalent) unconditionally for T1 PRs and is a hard gate within that job; the T1 Proof Gate check-run reported SUCCESS on PR #1213 at the accepted head, so this CI-run test:db execution did occur and passed, even though it was not separately re-invoked by the executor in the local session. See t1_proof_gate below for the correction this replaces.

scope.summary: Raise the ratified base concurrency ceiling from 6 (2 Claude + 4 Codex) to 10 (4 Claude + 6 Codex) active lanes, and mechanically enforce the per-type distribution caps (Hygiene<=4, Governance<=3, Delivery/UI<=1-per-app, Verification<=1-per-target) that were previously prose-only. This continuation adds no implementation change -- only branch/manifest/proof-doc repair.
scope.files_changed_source: Generated from `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD` in the continuation worktree, not typed by hand (PM review correction).

non_goals: merge_serialized_max unchanged -- merge-train serialization is a real architectural constraint, untouched
12-14 lane trial stays disabled (enabled: false) -- not enabled by this lane
UTV2-1472 (Claude parallel dispatch) not folded in -- separate companion issue

scripts/ops/execution-state.ts / merge-risk.ts / lane-maximizer.ts's own '?? 2' / '?? 4' fallback defaults (config-load-failure-only dead code path) left unchanged -- pre-existing minor gap, out of scope
No implementation redesign in this continuation -- the accepted code from PR #1213 is carried forward unchanged; only branch, lane-manifest, lease, and proof-doc metadata were repaired

docs/governance/CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.json.type_caps: new field: { hygiene: 4, governance: 3, delivery-ui: { max_per_app: 1 }, verification: { max_per_target: 1 } }
docs/governance/CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.json.trial: expired 8-lane trial replaced with a disabled 14-lane trial (claude:5, codex:9, allowed_until:null)

safety_rationale.why_10_lanes_is_safe: Audited: total/executor caps were self-imposed policy numbers with no external mechanical constraint. The two real constraints (merge-train serialization, WSL2 full-verify semaphore) are both decoupled from active-lane count and untouched by this change.
safety_rationale.why_type_caps_needed_real_code_not_prose: PR #1213 automated review (Codex, P2 finding) and PM review round 1 both identified that ops:lane-start enforced total/executor/singleton/forbidden-combination limits but had no machine-readable per-type distribution cap -- the policy doc's Hygiene<=4/Governance<=3/Delivery-UI-1-per-app/Verification-1-per-target claims were aspirational, not enforced. A 5th-10th same-safe-type lane could be admitted whenever executor slots and file scopes allowed it.
safety_rationale.identity_derivation_design: Delivery/UI app identity is derived deterministically from the lane's own file_scope_lock against the 4 canonical app roots already defined in LANE_TAXONOMY.md SS7 (deriveDeliveryUiApp()) -- never inferred from free-form text. Verification target identity has no reliable existing field (a verification lane's own issue_id is not necessarily the issue it verifies), so a new validated verification_target manifest field was added, schema_version-2-gated, mirroring the exact enforcement shape already used for model_routing (UTV2-1526) -- same deletion-attack protection, same required/forbidden/malformed validation.
safety_rationale.fail_closed_design: Both new checks fail closed: a Delivery/UI lane whose scope doesn't map to exactly one app root is refused, not guessed (delivery_ui_app_undetermined). An active Verification lane whose target can't be determined (legacy manifest with no verification_target) blocks every new Verification lane start until resolved (verification_target_undetermined_conflict) -- treated as a conflict, not a silent pass.
safety_rationale.trial_isolation: type_caps is read unconditionally from base config in getEffectiveConfig() (both the trial-active and trial-inactive return branches spread ...config, and neither branch overrides type_caps) -- a future 12-14 lane trial widens total/executor headroom only, never the per-type distribution caps. Verified by a dedicated adversarial test.

runtime_proof.note: No file in this diff's scope touches DB schema, DB queries, or Supabase-connected code -- governance config/docs and TypeScript ops-orchestration tooling (lane-lifecycle scripts, unit tests with in-memory fixtures) only. R-level check independently confirms no DB/runtime rule is triggered by this diff's file paths.
runtime_proof.t1_proof_gate_correction: PM review caught an inaccuracy in an earlier round of this document: 'pnpm test:db was NOT run for this lane' was true only for the local executor session, and was written in a way that could be read as claiming no test:db execution occurred anywhere for this PR. That was wrong. CI's "T1 Proof Gate" job (.github/workflows/proof-gate.yml, job t1-proof) runs a hard-gated "C2 — live-DB proof execution" step that executes `pnpm ci:db-smoke` (the pnpm test:db equivalent) unconditionally for every T1 PR unless a narrow schema-parity exception applies (it does not here -- this manifest has no runtime_proof_kind field). The T1 Proof Gate check-run reported SUCCESS on PR #1213 at the accepted head, meaning C2 (and therefore pnpm test:db) genuinely ran and passed in CI. That CI execution is the applicable T1 runtime-verification step for this diff and is not being denied here.
runtime_proof.test_db_statement: R-level check (file-path-triggered, not tier-label-driven) independently confirms no rule requiring a local pnpm test:db invocation was triggered by this diff's specific files. The executor did not separately re-run pnpm test:db locally for that reason. Separately and correctly: CI's T1 Proof Gate DID execute pnpm test:db (via ci:db-smoke) as part of its own required C2 step, and it passed -- see t1_proof_gate_correction above.

round_5_fixes.source: Fresh Codex review triggered against the replacement PR #1215's initial head (27dcf5cf), both P2 severity, both real bugs in this issue's own round-2/round-3 code -- not pre-existing accepted code, and not a redesign of the accepted implementation.
round_5_fixes.delivery_ui_fail_open_on_undetermined_ac: FIXED -- the per-app conflict check compared the incoming lane's derived app against each active Delivery/UI lane's derived app, but an active lane whose own file_scope_lock could not be reduced to one canonical app (deriveDeliveryUiApp() returns null) always compared as non-conflicting (`null === incomingApp` is false), silently admitting a same-app collision. Codex cited a live example: the workspace's own active UTV2-1396 manifest has this exact shape. Fixed by mirroring the identical fail-closed treatment already applied to undetermined active Verification lanes -- an undetermined active Delivery/UI lane now blocks any new Delivery/UI lane start (delivery_ui_app_undetermined_conflict) rather than being silently skipped. 2 new tests.
round_5_fixes.verification_target_normalization_discar: FIXED -- requireIssueId() normalizes (uppercases) its input and returns the normalized value, but the early-validation call discarded that return value, so a lower-case --verification-target (e.g. "utv2-123") passed the early check via requireIssueId's own internal uppercasing, then reached createManifest's case-sensitive ISSUE_PATTERN test as the original lower-case string and failed AFTER createBranchAndWorktree/reserveLease had already run -- the exact orphaned-state case the early check exists to prevent. Fixed by declaring verificationTargetFlag with `let` and reassigning it to requireIssueId()'s normalized return value, so every downstream consumer sees the canonical form. 1 new test.

round_6_fix.source: A second confirmation Codex review triggered against round 5's head (ba5d67c9) surfaced 1 more real finding, again in this issue's own code (round 5's own normalization fix), not pre-existing accepted code.
round_6_fix.verification_target_accepted_uni_prefix: FIXED -- round 5's normalization fix used the general requireIssueId() helper, which accepts both UTV2-### and UNI-### (ISSUE_PATTERN), but verification_target is documented UTV2-### only in lane_manifest_v1.schema.json's pattern ("^UTV2-[0-9]+$") and LANE_MANIFEST_SPEC.md SS16 -- a UNI-### verification target would silently pass validation (both the CLI early check and createManifest's own check, which had the identical gap) while disagreeing with the documented JSON schema. Fixed: new VERIFICATION_TARGET_PATTERN (/^UTV2-\d+$/, stricter than ISSUE_PATTERN) and an exported requireVerificationTarget() helper in shared.ts, used consistently in lane-start.ts's CLI check, createManifest(), and validateManifest(). 2 new tests.

round_7_fix.source: A third confirmation Codex review triggered against round 6's head (103dce4b) surfaced 2 more findings: one real functional regression, one advisory-quality concern.
round_7_fix.codex_dispatch_broke_verification_lanes: FIXED -- scripts/codex-dispatch.ts's runLaneStart() built its ops:lane-start invocation without ever passing --verification-target, so any Codex-executed lane_type:verification dispatch would fail closed with verification_target_missing before the lane could be created. Round 5's fix in lane-maximizer.ts only covered the *advisory suggested command text*, never the actual Codex execution path in codex-dispatch.ts. Fixed: codex-dispatch.ts now computes verificationTarget = laneType === 'verification' ? (explicitVerificationTarget ?? issueId) : undefined (same default-with-override pattern as --lane-type/--model-profile in that file) and threads it through runLaneStart() and the --dry-run preview. New regression test in codex-dispatch.test.ts.
round_7_fix.lane_maximizer_advisory_target_guess_def: NOT FIXED HERE, DEFERRED -- if a candidate's real intended verification target differs from its own issue_id, lane-maximizer's advisory default guess could differ from the real target, so a blindly-run suggested command might not trip the per-target cap against a genuinely conflicting active lane. This is an advisory-quality concern, not a mechanical safety gap: ops:lane-start's checkConcurrencyLimits() remains the fail-closed authority and correctly enforces whatever target it is actually given -- the risk is confined to the *suggestion* being potentially wrong, already documented as a known limitation in lane-maximizer.ts's own code comment ("this script cannot know the real target with certainty"). Already covered in spirit by the pre-existing follow-up UTV2-1535. Replied on the PR explaining this deferral rather than silently fixing or silently resolving; left unresolved for PM visibility.

round_8_fix.source: Final pre-packet CI sweep on PR #1215 (this document's own closeout step, not a Codex review) found Branch Discipline Guard (workflow: branch-discipline-guard.yml, job "Check issue references") failing. It scans PR title + PR body + every commit reachable via GitHub's pulls.listCommits and requires exactly one distinct UTV2-###/UNI-### ID (the branch's own) across all of them combined -- zero tolerance, no allowlist.
round_8_fix.first_pass_fix: Reworded the 4 commits this continuation itself authored strictly after accepted_pr_head_sha (the round-5 fix, the round-7 fix, and the 2 origin/main-merge commits) via `git filter-branch --msg-filter` restricted to the exact range accepted_pr_head_sha..HEAD (non-interactive, no -i flag) -- message-only, every commit tree/diff byte-identical before and after, accepted_pr_head_sha confirmed unchanged and still an ancestor. This removed this continuation's OWN descriptive stray references (UTV2-1396, UTV2-1484, UTV2-1535 that appeared only in this continuation's own commit-message prose). Pushed as 5a48c51d.
round_8_fix.deeper_finding_after_first_pass: Re-running the real GitHub check post-push revealed the first-pass fix did not change the check's verdict at all, and for a reason distinct from the already-known UTV2-1526 case: the two origin/main merge commits (9b66517a, 27dcf5cf) don't just carry their OWN descriptive message text (which round 8's first pass already cleaned) -- merging origin/main made every individual commit from whichever OTHER lanes had already landed on main at that moment into ancestors of this branch too, and each of those commits carries its own native issue-ID text in its own message (e.g. 93fe26ed 'feat(ops): UTV2-1484 Command Center Governance Board Read-Only API Surface (#1208)', a genuine, already-shipped commit from an unrelated lane, now reachable as this branch's own ancestor). GitHub's pulls.listCommits includes these individual commits directly, not just the merge commit's own message. Confirmed via `gh api repos/griff843/Unit-Talk-v2/pulls/1215/commits --paginate`: UTV2-1396 (2 commits: adfbd333, 4938eae7), UTV2-1484 (3 commits: 93fe26ed, 8ba94e40, d092438e), UTV2-1485 (2 commits: f4226ea0, 8ba94e40), UTV2-1532 (2 commits: 445192e3, 607a5696) -- all pulled in via the two merges, none of them this continuation's own authored text. Additionally the set observed keeps drifting (it grew, not shrank, between the pre-round-8 check and the post-round-8 check) because GitHub's PR-commit comparison recomputes dynamically against the CURRENT origin/main tip, and origin/main keeps advancing while this PR sits open -- the longer this PR remains unmerged, the more of these already-baked-in individual commits are likely to surface as 'ahead', not fewer.
round_8_fix.why_this_cannot_be_further_fixed_unilate: Removing these merged-in individual commits from the branch's ancestry requires either (a) not merging origin/main at all -- already done twice, cannot be undone without also undoing the R-level-check-stability fix those merges were performed for -- or (b) a rebase of this branch's unique commits onto a fresh origin/main, which would assign a NEW SHA to every commit including accepted_pr_head_sha itself, destroying the exact-history-preservation guarantee that is this continuation's core integrity property and that every prior round's SHA-bound proof claim depends on. Both of those are decisions bigger than a mechanical commit-message cleanup and were not authorized by the continuation directive, which explicitly required 'no cherry-pick/reconstruction, preserve exact history.' No further git-history surgery was attempted past this point.
round_8_fix.combined_with_pre_existing_1526: UTV2-1526 (commit 343735ba, an ancestor of accepted_pr_head_sha itself, citing the pre-existing model_routing/UTV2-1526 enforcement pattern as design precedent in the ALREADY-ACCEPTED PR #1213 history) has the same root cause in kind: content that legitimately belongs to already-reviewed, already-shipped work, now unavoidably reachable as this branch's ancestry. PR #1213 (the original, still-open, already-accepted PR, which never merged origin/main) independently fails this same Branch Discipline Guard check too -- confirming the underlying tension (preserve-exact-history vs. zero-tolerance-cross-reference-scanning) predates this continuation and is not something the continuation introduced.
round_8_fix.actual_ci_result: Branch Discipline Guard remains RED at the final head (ccd44673), now failing with 'All PR issue references must match branch issue UTV2-1533; found UTV2-1396, UTV2-1484, UTV2-1485, UTV2-1526, UTV2-1532, UTV2-1533' -- reduced by 1 (UTV2-1535 removed) from the original 6-ID set, with the remaining 5 all structurally unfixable without either abandoning history preservation or abandoning the origin/main-merge strategy used to keep the R-level check stable. This is a genuine, mechanical, PM-arbitrable finding: the round-8 fix demonstrably removed everything within this agent's unilateral authority to remove.
